parts.text:
Explore the opencode codebase and environment. I need to understand:

1. Read /Users/ubuntu/1-project/idea0015-trae-plugin-cc/.opencode/ directory in full - package.json, plugins dir, etc.
2. Check if there's an opencode binary installed. Run `opencode --help` to see its CLI interface.
3. Search for opencode's plugin/skill/extension system:
   - Look at /Users/ubuntu/.agents/ directory structure  
   - Read any SKILL.md files that describe how skills/plugins work
   - Read the find-skills skill at /Users/ubuntu/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md
4. Check the opencode source code if available - look for how commands, tools, plugins, MCP tools are registered.
5. Look at how slash commands or equivalent commands work in opencode vs Claude Code.
6. Check if opencode has an equivalent to Claude Code's hooks/hooks.json system.
7. Check if opencode uses .mcp.json for MCP tool registration.

I need a complete understanding of opencode's extension architecture so I can plan how to port a Claude Code plugin to it.

Return:
- opencode plugin/skill/tool extension model
- How commands are defined and invoked
- How MCP tools are declared and used  
- How hooks/lifecycle events work (if at all)
- File structure expectations for a plugin/skill
- Key differences from Claude Code's plugin system

The user wants me to explore the opencode codebase and environment to understand its extension architecture. Let me break this down into multiple parallel searches:

1. Read the .opencode/ directory
2. Check opencode binary and CLI
3. Explore .agents/ directory and SKILL.md files
4. Search for opencode source code
5. Look for slash commands or equivalent
6. Check for hooks system
7. Check .mcp.json usage

Let me start with multiple parallel operations.
I'll conduct a thorough exploration of the opencode codebase and environment. Let me start with multiple parallel searches.

input.command: opencode --help 2>&1 || echo "opencode not found in PATH"

state.output:
в Ђ                                в–„     
в–€в–Ђв–Ђв–€ в–€в–Ђв–Ђв–€ в–€в–Ђв–Ђв–€ в–€в–Ђв–Ђв–„ в–€в–Ђв–Ђв–Ђ в–€в–Ђв–Ђв–€ в–€в–Ђв–Ђв–€ в–€в–Ђв–Ђв–€
в–€  в–€ в–€  в–€ в–€в–Ђв–Ђв–Ђ в–€  в–€ в–€    в–€  в–€ в–€  в–€ в–€в–Ђв–Ђв–Ђ
в–Ђв–Ђв–Ђв–Ђ в–€в–Ђв–Ђв–Ђ в–Ђв–Ђв–Ђв–Ђ в–Ђ  в–Ђ в–Ђв–Ђв–Ђв–Ђ в–Ђв–Ђв–Ђв–Ђ в–Ђв–Ђв–Ђв–Ђ в–Ђв–Ђв–Ђв–Ђ

Commands:
  opencode completion          generate shell completion script
  opencode acp                 start ACP (Agent Client Protocol) server
  opencode mcp                 manage MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers
  opencode [project]           start opencode tui                                          [default]
  opencode attach <url>        attach to a running opencode server
  opencode run [message..]     run opencode with a message
  opencode debug               debugging and troubleshooting tools
  opencode providers           manage AI providers and credentials                   [aliases: auth]
  opencode agent               manage agents
  opencode upgrade [target]    upgrade opencode to the latest or a specific version
  opencode uninstall           uninstall opencode and remove all related files
  opencode serve               starts a headless opencode server
  opencode web                 start opencode server and open web interface
  opencode models [provider]   list all available models
  opencode stats               show token usage and cost statistics
  opencode export [sessionID]  export session data as JSON
  opencode import <file>       import session data from JSON file or URL
  opencode github              manage GitHub agent
  opencode pr <number>         fetch and checkout a GitHub PR branch, then run opencode
  opencode session             manage sessions
  opencode plugin <module>     install plugin and update config                      [aliases: plug]
  opencode db                  database tools

Positionals:
  project  path to start opencode in                                                        [string]

Options:
  -h, --help         show help                                                             [boolean]
  -v, --version      show version number                                                   [boolean]
      --print-logs   print logs to stderr                                                  [boolean]
      --log-level    log level                  [string] [choices: "DEBUG", "INFO", "WARN", "ERROR"]
      --pure         run without external plugins                                          [boolean]
      --port         port to listen on                                         [number] [default: 0]
      --hostname     hostname to listen on                           [string] [default: "127.0.0.1"]
      --mdns         enable mDNS service discovery (defaults hostname to 0.0.0.0)
                                                                          [boolean] [default: false]
      --mdns-domain  custom domain name for mDNS service (default: opencode.local)
                                                                [string] [default: "opencode.local"]
      --cors         additional domains to allow for CORS                      [array] [default: []]
  -m, --model        model to use in the format of provider/model                           [string]
  -c, --continue     continue the last session                                             [boolean]
  -s, --session      session id to continue                                                 [string]
      --fork         fork the session when continuing (use with --continue or --session)   [boolean]
      --prompt       prompt to use                                                          [string]
      --agent        agent to use                                                           [string]

  opencode serve               starts a headless opencode server

      --mdns         enable mDNS service discovery (defaults hostname to 0.0.0.0)
                                                                          [boolean] [default: false]
      --mdns-domain  custom domain name for mDNS service (default: opencode.local)
                                                                [string] [default: "opencode.local"]
      --cors         additional domains to allow for CORS                      [array] [default: []]
  -m, --model        model to use in the format of provider/model                           [string]
  -c, --continue     continue the last session                                             [boolean]
  -s, --session      session id to continue                                                 [string]
